title: Circular dependency (schema type change triggers cascading API changes)
description: The products.price column changes from INT to DECIMAL(10,2). Because the orders API embeds product data in its response, this schema change cascades into a breaking API change: the embedded product.price field changes from integer to number in the response. Separately, a new total_price DECIMAL column is added to orders, which is a safe additive change at both schema and API layers. The key complexity here is the cross-table cascade: a type change in products directly propagates a breaking change into the orders API response through the embedded product object.

schema_changes.reason: Widening from INT to DECIMAL is backward-compatible at the database level. All existing integer values fit in DECIMAL(10,2).
Adding a nullable DECIMAL column to orders is a safe additive change. Existing rows get NULL and existing INSERTs that omit this column continue to work.
The type change in products.price cascades into the orders API response because product data is embedded in the order response object.

api_changes.reason: The embedded product.price field changes from integer to number due to the cascading schema type change. Consumers deserializing product.price into an int field will fail at runtime.
Adding a new top-level field to the order response is backward-compatible for lenient deserializers.
